content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Art Pulaski 
Executive Secretary 
San Mateo County Central 
Labor Council 
300 Eighth Avenue, suite 1 
San Mateo, CA 
94401 
Dear Mr. Pulaski: 
September 13, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 88-355 
Your letter requesting advice concerning the recently 
enacted campaign finance reform initiatives (Government Code 
Section 85100, et ~) was received on September 9, 1988 by 
the Fair Political Practices commission. 
If you have any 
questions about your advice request, you may contact John 
McLean, an attorney in the Legal Division, directly at 
(916) 322-5901. 
Because of the delayed operative date of most of the 
provisions of these initiatives, and the numerous inquiries we 
are receiving relative to implementation, we are attempting to 
respond to questions regarding interpretation in an organized 
and efficient fashion. 
Due to the volume of requests and 
complexity of the issues involved, we will answer your question 
in a timely manner, but not within the usual twenty-one working 
days. 
(Government Code section 83114(b).) 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
You also should be aware 
that your letter and our response are public records which may 
be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for 
disclosure. 
GWB:plh:3 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


September 7, 1988 
F.P.P.C. 
428 IIJII Street 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Gentlemen: 
Please send us advice regarding the application of new 
regulations per passage of June ballot measures on 
Political Action Committees as it pertains to our 
organization. 
We are an independent recipient labor committee. 
(I. D. No. 743-614). 
Specifically, must we spend all funds in the year they 
were raised? 
I would appreciate a written response as soon as 
possible. 
Thank you. 
Executive Secretary 
ope 3 afl cio 174 


State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
: File 
Date 
October 28, 1988 
From 
: 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
John McLean 
Subject: T88355 
Spoke today with Art Pulaski, Executive Secretary of the 
San Mateo County Central Labor Council. 
Explained the 
segregation process established by regs. 18536 et. seq. and 
told him I would send the regs. 
He indicated that if I would 
send the regs., he would not need any further written response.
